About This Project

This project presents an interactive map of logistics infrastructure across the United Kingdom, using spatial visualisation to examine patterns of concentration and absence at multiple scales. Facilities are represented through complementary density methods, including continuous density surfaces and hexagonal aggregation, with individual locations revealed at higher zoom levels.

The interface supports multiple analytical views, allowing users to filter and switch between different logistics brands and facility subsets. Rather than providing a definitive assessment of service provision or performance, the map functions as an exploratory tool for examining logistics networks, regional structure, and infrastructural inequality, while illustrating the strengths and limitations of different approaches to representing spatial density.

Data sources: Logistics facility locations are compiled from OpenStreetMap (© OpenStreetMap contributors), accessed and processed for research and visualisation purposes. Base map and rendering are provided by Mapbox.

Created & Visualized by Arthur Zhang
